title: Understanding the Boiler Upgrade Scheme
subtitle: The grant for air source heat pumps: who qualifies and what's involved
excerpt: The Boiler Upgrade Scheme (BUS) gives off the cost of an air source or ground source heat pump in England and Wales. The grant goes to the MCS-certified installer and is deducted from your quote. Eligibility has three main gates: geography, property type, and EPC condition.
sourceNotes: OFGEM Boiler Upgrade Scheme guidance (ofgem.gov.uk/check-if-energy-grant-available). BUS eligibility criteria updated November 2023. MCS (Microgeneration Certification Scheme) installer register.

glossaryTerms.definition: A device that moves heat from a cold outdoor source to a warm indoor space using a refrigerant cycle. Air source (ASHP) uses outdoor air; ground source (GSHP) uses ground-loop pipes. Eligible for the BUS grant.

content.text: The Boiler Upgrade Scheme (BUS) pays toward the cost of an air source or ground source

. The grant is paid directly to the MCS-certified installer who deducts it from their quote. You do not claim it yourself, you simply pay the reduced price. The grant is available until 31 March 2028 under current policy.
Geography: England and Wales only. Scotland has its own scheme (Home Energy Scotland Heat Pump Grant).
Property: existing properties only. New-build homes are excluded.

EPC: the property must have a valid EPC with no outstanding recommendations for loft insulation or cavity wall insulation, unless a valid exemption applies (e.g. the cavity is unsuitable for fill, or there is no loft).
Prior heat pump: a property cannot receive a second BUS grant for the same technology.
Ground source heat pump (GSHP), including water source: .
Biomass boiler: (rural properties with no gas grid connection only).

Only MCS-certified installers can access the BUS. They apply for the voucher before installation begins. The voucher has a three-month validity. If the installation is not completed within the voucher period, a new application is required. Installers are responsible for confirming EPC eligibility, ask to see this confirmed in writing before signing a contract.

After installation, the installer submits a post-installation report to OFGEM and claims the grant from the scheme. You receive a reduced invoice. You are also entitled to a Building Regulations Completion Certificate (for the electrical work involved) and MCS installation certificate, keep both for the EPC update and any future sale.
